title: Apple Pudding or Afternoon Tart
excerpt: Riette and Anton are visiting us. While cooking, a craving for apple tart hits me. There and then we decide that apple pudding is just the right thing for Hopefield cold. The recipe is adapted for what we have in the pantry .( remember our shops close 12 o'clock …

seo.title: Apple Pudding or Afternoon Tart - Marinda Kook

html:
Riette and Anton are visiting us. While cooking, a craving for apple tart hits me. There and then we decide that apple pudding is just the right thing for Hopefield cold. The recipe is adapted for what we have in the pantry .( remember our shops close at 12 o'clock on a Sunday afternoon)

BATTER:

  1. 250 ml sugar
  2. 60ml butter or margarine
  3. 3 eggs
  4. 5ml salt
  5. 250 ml self-raising flour
  6. 2 ml baking powder
  7. 60 ml milk
  8. 1 tin (385g) pie apples
  9. 125ml raisins soaked in 100ml brandy
  • beat the butter and sugar together until pale yellow
  • add the eggs one at a time, beating continuously
  • now add the self-raising flour, salt, baking powder and milk to the egg mixture
  • take the raisins out of the brandy (drink the brandy or pour it away) and stir the raisins into your batter
  • grease an ovenproof dish and pour the batter into the ovenproof dish.
  • arrange the apples in the batter – press them in nice and deep – just with your fingers
  • bake for 40 minutes at 190 degrees Celsius

SYRUP

Heat in a pot on the stove: 250 ml sugar, 250 ml cream and 5ml caramel essence – stir until sugar has dissolved

As soon as the pudding comes out of the oven, pour the hot syrup over the pudding. I also sprinkled finely chopped flaked almonds over it.

Enjoy it with stiffly whipped cream, ice cream or custard. If there's any left over, you can have it with your afternoon coffee.
